---
title: "Technical Report"
jupyter: utah-housing-project
---

## Introduction

<!-- TODO: Write 3–5 sentences covering:
     (1) the research question / motivation,
     (2) the outcome variable and geographic/temporal scope,
     (3) the high-level modeling goals (EDA, model selection, evaluation). -->

[Introduction goes here.]

---

## Data Source and Methodology

<!-- TODO: Replace each numbered item with specifics for your project. -->

1. **Data acquisition**
   - [Describe how data was collected, e.g. ACS 5-year estimates via the Census API using the `utah-housing` package.]

2. **Cleaning pipeline**
   - [Describe cleaning steps: missing-value handling, column derivation, filtering, etc.]

3. **Analysis workflow**
   - *EDA:* [Describe exploratory plots and summaries.]
   - *Model selection:* [Describe the approach, e.g. best-subsets by AIC and LASSO.]
   - *Model assessment:* [Describe evaluation method, e.g. 5-fold cross-validated PMSE.]

4. **Tooling and reproducibility**
   - Primary libraries: `pandas`, `numpy`, `matplotlib`, `seaborn`, `statsmodels`, `patsy`, `scikit-learn`
   - Environment and packaging: see `pyproject.toml`

---

## EDA

```{python}
#| include: false
#| eval: false
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from utah_housing import OUTCOME, BASE_PREDICTORS, COMPLEX_PREDICTORS

# TODO: Update path if data file lives elsewhere
df = pd.read_csv("data/utah_housing.csv")
df["county_name"] = df["NAME"].str.extract(r",\s*(.+?)\s+County", expand=False)
df = df.dropna(subset=[OUTCOME]).copy()
```

```{python}
#| eval: false
# TODO: Replace variable names with your actual predictors.
# The 2x2 layout below mirrors the example project structure.

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(12, 10))
fig.suptitle("EDA: Outcome vs Selected Predictors", fontsize=16, fontweight="bold")

# Plot 1 (top-left): Scatterplot — outcome vs predictor 1
# axes[0, 0].scatter(df["[predictor_1]"], df[OUTCOME], alpha=0.5, s=20, color="steelblue")
# axes[0, 0].set_xlabel("[Predictor 1 label]")
# axes[0, 0].set_ylabel(OUTCOME.replace("_", " ").title())
# axes[0, 0].set_title("Outcome vs [Predictor 1]", fontweight="bold")
# axes[0, 0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Plot 2 (top-right): Scatterplot — outcome vs predictor 2
# axes[0, 1].scatter(df["[predictor_2]"], df[OUTCOME], alpha=0.5, s=20, color="coral")
# axes[0, 1].set_xlabel("[Predictor 2 label]")
# axes[0, 1].set_ylabel(OUTCOME.replace("_", " ").title())
# axes[0, 1].set_title("Outcome vs [Predictor 2]", fontweight="bold")
# axes[0, 1].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Plot 3 (bottom-left): Scatterplot — outcome vs predictor 3
# axes[1, 0].scatter(df["[predictor_3]"], df[OUTCOME], alpha=0.5, s=20)
# axes[1, 0].set_xlabel("[Predictor 3 label]")
# axes[1, 0].set_ylabel(OUTCOME.replace("_", " ").title())
# axes[1, 0].set_title("Outcome vs [Predictor 3]", fontweight="bold")
# axes[1, 0].grid(True, alpha=0.3)

# Plot 4 (bottom-right): Boxplot — outcome by a categorical variable (e.g. county)
# counties_of_interest = ["[county_1]", "[county_2]", "[county_3]"]
# sub_df = df[df["county_name"].isin(counties_of_interest)]
# sub_df.boxplot(column=OUTCOME, by="county_name", ax=axes[1, 1])
# axes[1, 1].set_xlabel("County")
# axes[1, 1].set_ylabel(OUTCOME.replace("_", " ").title())
# axes[1, 1].set_title("Outcome by County", fontweight="bold")
# axes[1, 1].get_figure().suptitle("")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
#| eval: false
# TODO: Add trend-over-time plot (statewide median of outcome by year).

# yearly = (
#     df.groupby("year")[OUTCOME]
#     .median()
#     .reset_index()
# )
# fig2, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 4))
# ax.plot(yearly["year"], yearly[OUTCOME], marker="o")
# ax.set_xlabel("Year")
# ax.set_ylabel(OUTCOME.replace("_", " ").title())
# ax.set_title(f"Statewide Median {OUTCOME.replace('_', ' ').title()} Over Time")
# ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
# plt.show()
```

---

## Link to Streamlit App

<!-- TODO: Replace the URL once your Streamlit app is deployed. -->

[Link to Streamlit app](https://your-app-url.streamlit.app/)

---

## Analysis

```{python}
#| eval: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false

import itertools
import statsmodels.formula.api as smf
from patsy import dmatrices
from sklearn.model_selection import KFold
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression, LassoCV, Lasso
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
from sklearn.pipeline import Pipeline
from sklearn.metrics import mean_squared_error

# TODO: Update cat_vars to reflect categorical columns in your dataset (e.g. county, year).
# cat_vars = ["county_name"]
# for c in cat_vars:
#     if c in df.columns:
#         df[c] = df[c].astype("category")

# TODO: Update candidates to the columns you want to consider as predictors.
# candidates = COMPLEX_PREDICTORS

# ----------------------------
# Best subsets selection via AIC
# ----------------------------
# best_aic = np.inf
# best_formula = None
# best_model = None
#
# for k in range(1, len(candidates) + 1):
#     for subset in itertools.combinations(candidates, k):
#         terms = [f"C({v})" if v in cat_vars else v for v in subset]
#         formula = f"{OUTCOME} ~ " + " + ".join(terms)
#         try:
#             model = smf.ols(formula, data=df).fit()
#             if model.aic < best_aic:
#                 best_aic = model.aic
#                 best_formula = formula
#                 best_model = model
#         except Exception:
#             continue
#
# print("Best AIC:", best_aic)
# print("Best formula:", best_formula)
# print(best_model.summary())

# ----------------------------
# CV PMSE for best-AIC model
# ----------------------------
# y_aic, X_aic = dmatrices(best_formula, data=df, return_type="dataframe")
# y_aic = np.ravel(y_aic)
# kf = KFold(n_splits=5, shuffle=True, random_state=1)
# mses_aic = []
# for tr, te in kf.split(X_aic):
#     lr = LinearRegression(fit_intercept=False)
#     lr.fit(X_aic.iloc[tr], y_aic[tr])
#     preds = lr.predict(X_aic.iloc[te])
#     mses_aic.append(mean_squared_error(y_aic[te], preds))
# pmse_aic = np.mean(mses_aic)

# ----------------------------
# LASSO (lambda = 1-SE rule)
# ----------------------------
# full_formula = f"{OUTCOME} ~ " + " + ".join(
#     [f"C({v})" if v in cat_vars else v for v in candidates]
# )
# y_lasso, X_lasso = dmatrices(full_formula, data=df, return_type="dataframe")
# y_lasso = np.ravel(y_lasso)
#
# lasso_cv = Pipeline([
#     ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
#     ("lasso", LassoCV(cv=5, random_state=1))
# ])
# lasso_cv.fit(X_lasso, y_lasso)
#
# mse_path = lasso_cv.named_steps["lasso"].mse_path_.mean(axis=1)
# mse_std  = lasso_cv.named_steps["lasso"].mse_path_.std(axis=1)
# idx_min  = np.argmin(mse_path)
# mse_1se  = mse_path[idx_min] + mse_std[idx_min]
# idx_1se  = np.where(mse_path <= mse_1se)[0][-1]
# alpha_1se = lasso_cv.named_steps["lasso"].alphas_[idx_1se]
#
# lasso_1se = Pipeline([
#     ("scaler", StandardScaler()),
#     ("lasso", Lasso(alpha=alpha_1se))
# ])
# lasso_1se.fit(X_lasso, y_lasso)
#
# coef = lasso_1se.named_steps["lasso"].coef_
# selected = X_lasso.columns[coef != 0]
# print("LASSO selected variables:", list(selected))

# ----------------------------
# Refit OLS on LASSO-selected variables
# ----------------------------
# selected_vars = set()
# for col in selected:
#     if col.startswith("C("):
#         var = col.split("[")[0].replace("C(", "").replace(")", "")
#         selected_vars.add(f"C({var})")
#     else:
#         selected_vars.add(col)
# selected_vars = sorted(selected_vars)
# lasso_formula = f"{OUTCOME} ~ " + " + ".join(selected_vars)
# lasso_refit = smf.ols(lasso_formula, data=df).fit()
# print(lasso_refit.summary())

# ----------------------------
# CV PMSE for LASSO-selected model
# ----------------------------
# y_lasso2, X_lasso2 = dmatrices(lasso_formula, data=df, return_type="dataframe")
# y_lasso2 = np.ravel(y_lasso2)
# mses_lasso = []
# for tr, te in kf.split(X_lasso2):
#     lr = LinearRegression(fit_intercept=False)
#     lr.fit(X_lasso2.iloc[tr], y_lasso2[tr])
#     preds = lr.predict(X_lasso2.iloc[te])
#     mses_lasso.append(mean_squared_error(y_lasso2[te], preds))
# pmse_lasso = np.mean(mses_lasso)
#
# print(f"Best Subsets (AIC) PMSE : {pmse_aic:.3f}")
# print(f"LASSO (1SE) PMSE        : {pmse_lasso:.3f}")
```

---

## Conclusion

<!-- TODO: Write 3–5 paragraphs covering:
     (1) summary of model comparison results (AIC best-subsets vs LASSO),
     (2) key predictors and their direction/magnitude,
     (3) cross-validation PMSE comparison and what it implies,
     (4) limitations (data quality, potential bias, model assumptions),
     (5) future work (additional variables, nonlinear models, spatial effects, deployment). -->

[Conclusion goes here.]
